sections.paragraph:
καταπατέω fut. καταπατήσω; 1 aor. κατεπάτησα. Pass.: fut. 3 sg. καταπατηθήσεται Is 25:10; 28:3; aor. κατεπατήθην; pf. 3 pl. καταπεπάτηνται 1 Macc 3:51 (s. πατέω; Hom., Hdt., Thu.+; SIG 1169, 115; pap, LXX; TestZeb 3:3; JosAs 29:3; SyrBar 13:11 [?]; Joseph.; Ar. 4, 3; Just., D. 76, 6 [for πατεῖν Lk 10:19]; Mel., P. 102, 782)

① to tread so heavily as to injure, trample under foot.

ⓐ trample τὶ ἔν τινι someth. with (under) someth. Mt 7:6 (of swine πατέω: Ananius Lyricus [VI b.c.]: Fgm. 5, 4 AnthLG Diehl3 [in Athen. 7, 282b]).—Pass. (Diod S 25, 3, 1) be trampled under foot Mt 5:13; Lk 8:5.

ⓑ tread upon of a milling crowd (Arrian, Anab. 2, 11, 3 ἀπʼ ἀλλήλων καταπατούμενοι=hard-pressed by each other, getting in each other’s way; Polyaenus 4, 3, 21 ὑπʼ ἀλλήλων καταπατούμενοι) ὥστε καταπατεῖν ἀλλήλους so that they stepped on one another Lk 12:1.

② to look on with scorn, trample under foot, treat with disdain fig. extension of mng. 1 (Il. 4, 157 [in tmesis]; Pla., Leg. 4, 714a τοὺς νόμους, Gorg. 484a; Epict. 1, 8, 10; Lucian, Lexiph. 23; 2 Macc 8:2; Hos 5:11; Jos., Bell. 4, 386.—Cp. the underlying reality in Diod S 33, 5, 3 τὰ τῶν θεῶν ἀγάλματα ὑβριστικῶς κατεπάτησαν) τὸν υἱὸν τοῦ θεοῦ Hb 10:29 (Artem. 4, 66 ἀνὴρ κατεπατήθη).—M-M. TW.

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. sg. = singular
  2. pf. = perfect
  3. pl. = plural

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestZeb TestZeb = Testament of Zebulon, s. Test12Patr—List 2

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

SyrBar SyrBar = Apocalypsis Syriaca Baruch—List 2

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Ar. Ar. = Aristides, apologist, II a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

Ananius Ananius , VI b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

AnthLG AnthLG = Anthologia Lyrica Graeca—List 5

Diehl Diehl , ed., s. AnthLG—List 5

Athen Athen , III a.d.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Arrian Arrian = Flavius Arrianus, I–II a.d.—List 5

mng. mng. = meaning(s)

Il. Il. = Iliad, s. Hom.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Artem Artem , onir., II a.d.—List 5

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 523
